heuristics.diagnosis: Pipeline underutilized. Check for cache misses, branch mispredictions, or long-latency instructions.
suggestions: Inspect l1d_miss_rate and l3_miss — high values indicate working set exceeds cache.
Check for unaligned memory accesses (expensive on POWER).
Verify VSX/AltiVec SIMD paths are used in hot loops rather than scalar fallbacks.
Review data structure layout for cache line alignment (128B on POWER).
Check NUMA locality — ensure memory is local to the executing core.

heuristics.diagnosis: Execution stalled on backend (memory, divides, long-latency ops).
suggestions: Look for division or modulo in hot paths — replace with shifts/masks where possible.
Check for false sharing on shared data structures (128B cache lines on POWER).

notes: PM_RUN_* events count only non-idle, non-stolen cycles/instructions — preferred over raw counters for accuracy in shared/virtualized environments.
POWER9/10 supports simultaneous counting of up to 6 PMU events per group. Plan event groups to avoid multiplexing.
Cache lines are 128 bytes on POWER (vs 64B on x86). Alignment matters more.
For DPDK: verify rte_memcpy uses the POWER-optimized (AltiVec) variant at compile time. Check RTE_ARCH_PPC_64 ifdefs.
Event names are lowercase on RHEL 8 / kernel 4.18 (e.g. pm_run_cyc not PM_RUN_CYC).
frontend_bound metric is omitted: PM_NO_INST_AVAIL is not accessible under RHEL 8 / kernel 4.18 without CAP_SYS_ADMIN. Use backend_bound as the primary stall metric on POWER9.
